turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Ground -->
  <path d="M40 410 C120 395, 200 395, 280 410 S440 425, 480 415" fill="none" stroke="#9aa3af" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9"/>

  <!-- Motion swooshes (to suggest running) -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#7dd3fc" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M120 320 C160 300, 205 295, 250 305"/>
    <path d="M90 345 C135 320, 190 315, 240 330" stroke-width="8" opacity="0.75"/>
    <path d="M65 370 C120 345, 180 340, 235 360" stroke-width="6" opacity="0.6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Runner (simple stick figure with dynamic pose) -->
  <!-- Tail/hat-like head accent omitted to avoid text -->
  <g stroke="#111827" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
    <!-- Head -->
    <circle cx="285" cy="235" r="30" fill="#f3f4f6" stroke="#111827"/>

    <!-- Torso -->
    <path d="M285 265 C280 310, 270 330, 250 355" fill="none"/>

    <!-- Arms -->
    <path d="M250 355 C235 330, 220 320, 195 310" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M250 355 C260 320, 275 300, 305 285" fill="none"/>

    <!-- Legs -->
    <path d="M250 355 C240 385, 250 410, 275 435" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M250 355 C235 385, 210 405, 185 418" fill="none"/>

    <!-- Slight bend lines for energy -->
    <path d="M275 435 C286 444, 294 452, 302 455" fill="none" stroke="#1d4ed8" opacity="0.9" stroke-width="6"/>
    <path d="M185 418 C170 428, 155 438, 145 445" fill="none" stroke="#1d4ed8" opacity="0.9" stroke-width="6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Foreground speed marks near feet -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#34d399" stroke-width="7" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M305 410 C335 395, 360 392, 390 400"/>
    <path d="M165 435 C190 420, 220 415, 250 420" opacity="0.65"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Drawing a running figure**

I need to represent the word "run" using only shapes and no text. A simple idea is a stick figure with motion lines, so I can create an SVG that's 512x512. I’ll sketch a ground line with a dynamic runner, making their arms and legs angled for motion. Adding arrows might help clarify movement. To emphasize the “run” concept, I'll include three swooshes behind to suggest speed. That should keep it clear and effective!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

message.reasoning:
**Interpreting a drawing**

I'm analyzing a drawing that looks like a stick figure. It's got a circular head and some lines for the body, which could represent someone running or diving. The circle at the top, along with lines radiating out, makes me think it's a swimmer or diver, especially with the splash effect around it. I want to come up with a one-word interpretation. My top possibilities include swimmer, diving, splash, water, and lifeguard. I'll aim for about 20 unique words.
